rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance.
Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban
No stale markers, no "fixed" retrospectives, no session biography.
Register match.
Rough-cut drafts for articles.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Not applicable — module file, not article draft.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Credit the original when paraphrasing a named technique.
Always "you", never "the student"; teacher framing stays out of body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 21: 'Last module you were the librarian.' — uses second person but the phrasing 'last module' addresses the student's past state, overlapping with check_student_facing.md §3 (no narrating student's present/past state).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Rephrase to avoid re-narrating student's just-lived phase: shift to artifact/module perspective or forward-looking stance. Consider: 'Last module built the librarian's work — searches, sources, memory. This module distributes the work across agents.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
Over-hedge detector.
Verbatim-frame-cite in reference artefact.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Not applicable — module file, not reference artifact.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cap practitioner attribution at one student-side mention per module.
Punchy framing keeps its punch; carve-out lives downstream.
Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project-level strategy docs, NOT in student-facing curriculum body.
Watch out for planting jargon too early.
No combative verbs about the agent in body prose.
Aspirational destination, never corrective gap.
Earn every technical term on first use.
Business-audience word ban (Bootstrap default).
Memory vs context — use deliberately.
Golden path only in the body.
Trust Claude's defaults for structure.
Q1/Q2/Q3 retro interview is banned at every module.
Don't smuggle another module's voice.
Don't ask the student to manually diff or spot-check Claude's output as error-catching.
Simulation personas never alone.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Not applicable — module file, no self-study simulations.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No "What research says" callout boxes
Student moves stay in conversation with the agent.
Specify which CLAUDE.md layer — every time.
No em-dashes. Simple sentences.
Section headers use command verbs when the section is about doing something.
Inline links use the target's title, never its filename.
Disambiguate shared-word collisions with Claude Code primitives on first student-authored use.
LLM-vs-agent-vs-Claude vocabulary discipline.
No time-of-day anchors in body prose.
Reflection beats stay invisible in the flow.
No meta-ask when the student has already decided.
`.md` is the default file type for student artifacts across the curriculum.
Cut NVA: true but not moving the student forward.
No overclaim of student verification or coverage
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 55: 'The evidence is what you just produced: the four agent prompts, the retrieval files, the synthesizer's briefing, the conversation. Claude reviews, rewrites the training-dir root rules file (`./CLAUDE.md`) in place, reports what changed.' — claims Claude reviews 'what you just produced' without scoping what 'review' includes. The Debrief section underscores this at line 55-56: student produces four prompts + files + briefing + conversation (multiple artifacts, multi-phase output), but the actual ask (lines 51-56) constrains to harsh audit + two seams minimum with evidence. The overclaim in line 55 states review scope without mentioning the constraint.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Scope the review claim to match the actual ask: 'Claude reviews the session and harshly audits the division-of-work rules, naming at least two handoff seams where problems surfaced.' Avoid 'what you just produced' scope-creep when the actual work is sampling (two seams, named examples, specific evidence).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No placeholders inside fenced blocks.
Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block.
Installed Claude Code Skills invoke by name, never by path.
Chain prompts by back-reference and deterministic path, never by invented alias.
Chained-prompt isolation — don't leak the downstream pipeline.
Prompt-chain context preservation — no session-resets mid-phase.
Multi-sample methods need structural enforcement in the prompt.
Markdown shout (`**bold**`, `*italic*`) inside fenced prompt blocks is dead weight in Claude Code.
Runtime-fork prompt blocks: paired divs, shared chip.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 13, 38, 42: 'subagentagent' tags appear in body prose (outside fenced prompts). Rule 10 applies to prompt-block forking, not body-prose terminology. The span tags mix runtime-fork markup (meant for prompts) into student-facing running text, creating visual noise and a misapplied pattern.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Remove the span tags from body prose. Prompts use <div class="rt-code"> / <div class="rt-cowork"> for dual-runtime blocks. Body mentions 'subagent' / 'agent' in plain text (no tags), trusting the student's runtime to understand which applies. Tags are for bifurcating prompt blocks, not for tagging every mention of the term.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Citation discipline — extend cite-or-flag scope, and quote the supporting sentence.
Niceness tax — name the edge or pin the line on regeneration.
Question-dump default — `in turn` is too soft for one-at-a-time rhythm.
Overwrite anxiety — name the overwrite default on regeneration.
Preamble before action — design around it, don't fight it.
Self-critique register, not defense, when Claude interrogates its own work.
Minimal hand-off + Claude self-augments for send-off prompts.
Grill before save — collaborative quality at artefact assembly.
Tell me what you wrote, not show me what you'll write.
Prompt blocks require maintainer approval before edit.
Artifact destinations are explicit subpaths, never naked filenames (Bootstrap-specific).
Don't restate session CWD inside the prompt body.
`subagent` is allowed in prompt blocks; explain why after the prompt.
Mood contract named.
Epistemic mood ≠ punitive mood.
Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
Strategy-fidelity check.
Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts.
Key Concepts shape — theme breadcrumbs, not a standalone section.
CTO-repeatable test.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Not applicable — module file, not sales copy.
